hero.subtitle: Most programs work the same way. This one doesn't. Strength programming unlike anything you've trained with before.

what.description: Most programs treat volume and intensity as a pair — when one goes up, the other comes down. StrongCode doesn't work that way. Volume and intensity are planned independently, and both are distributed across sessions, weeks, and months according to specific rules. That's what makes the programming look different from anything you've likely trained with before.
what.highlight: Most coaches can't tell you how much volume is in their plan — or what the average intensity of that volume is. StrongCode starts from the opposite end: volume and intensity are calculated first, then the program is built around those numbers.

expect.paragraph1: Expect steady progress over months, not dramatic peaks followed by burnout. You will rarely train to failure. You will lift heavy weights more often than you're used to — but leave the gym feeling capable of more.
expect.paragraph2: Can we promise exceptional results? No. Outcomes depend on many factors outside the program.
expect.paragraph3: Results depend on recovery. Sleep, stress management, and nutrition matter — we can't fix those. What we can do is make sure the programming isn't what holds you back.

step1.title: Fill out the survey
step1.description: We collect your 1RMs, recovery capacity, workload tolerance, and schedule. That's the input the calculations run on.

step2.title: We build your program
step2.description: The calculations set the parameters. The program itself is built by a coach — exercise selection, session structure, overall setup. Numbers plus experience from a process that has produced hundreds of plans.

step3.title: TRAIN. RECOVER. GET STRONGER.
step3.description: The minimum is a 2 months program. Longer preparatory phases usually produce better results. Three or more is where results tend to compound.

whoFor.description: StrongCode is not for beginners. It’s for lifters who already know how to train, have hit a plateau, and need programming that actually reflects where they are — not a generic template designed for everyone.
whoFor.point1: You have solid technique in the chosen lifts (powerlifts, kettlebell lifts)
whoFor.point2: Your progress has stalled on basic linear programming
whoFor.point3: You're done guessing what to do next
whoFor.point4: You want programming built around your actual numbers

tailored.title: INDIVIDUALIZED
tailored.description: Your program is built from your numbers — current 1RMs, training history, recovery capacity. Two lifters with different profiles get different programs. There are no templates.

unique.title: UNIQUE
unique.description: Volume and intensity change across sessions, weeks, and months instead of repeating the same work on autopilot.

longterm.title: Long Term
longterm.description: Fatigue is managed on purpose. Volume waves up and down so progress can continue without running your joints, motivation, and nervous system into the wall.

flexible.title: Flexible
flexible.description: Sessions can be rescheduled within a week. The system adapts to your life.

quote.text: Lift as heavy as possible, as much volume as possible, while remaining as fresh as possible.
quote.author: — Steve Freides

coach.name: Marcel Rand
coach.description: Software developer turned gym owner. Marcel spent years obsessing over object design and code optimization — then applied the same thinking to strength programming. He has been building training plans since, with the same chronic need to make them better.

ctaSection.description: Fill out the survey. We'll review your numbers and get back to you.

featuresPage.subtitle: What makes StrongCode different from other programs.

blocks.content: Your program is calculated and created from your numbers — current 1RMs, training history, recovery capacity, available time. Two lifters with different profiles will get different programs. There are no templates.
If you've only trained with fixed set × rep schemes, this will look different. Volume and intensity vary between sessions, weeks, and months — sometimes dramatically.

items: High variability of volume (between sessions, weeks, and months)
Volume and intensity are planned independently
Multiple intensity zones in each workout

blocks.content: In a single session, you might work at 65%, 80%, and 90%. The volume at each zone shifts from session to session and week to week according to distribution rules — not randomly.
Usually you won't do the same workout twice in a training block. The variability is built into the system.
If you provide correct data, you should never train to failure. This alone significantly lowers the probability of injuries.
Volume waves up and down across weeks. This prevents the accumulation of fatigue that eventually stops progress on most programs.

The same system, different parameters. Hypertrophy shifts the emphasis toward lower intensity zones and higher overall volume. Strength keeps the focus on heavy work. The structure stays the same — the settings change.
The easier weeks and easier sessions are already built in — volume drops, intensity mostly holds. No scheduled deloads. No grinding until you crash.

items: Flexible scheduling — sessions can be moved within a week, weeks within a month.
Variety of rep ranges at a given load removes psychological barriers associated with heavy weights.
Submaximal efforts. High volume without grinding.
Frequent heavy singles build confidence and consistency.
You stay close to your peak year-round. When you need to perform, the taper is short.

blocks.content: For beginners, progress is largely a function of weight on the bar. The programming is simple: keep volume steady, add weight. As you advance, progress depends more on volume and its distribution across sessions, weeks, and months. The more advanced you are, the more programming matters. That's where this system earns its value.
Linear and wave periodization tie volume and intensity together inversely. In StrongCode, they're independent. This allows combinations that traditional models can't produce.
Programming decisions are based on measurable variables across different time scales.

items: volume per year / month / week / session
average intensity per year / month / week / session
distribution of the volume between months / in a month / week / session
distribution of the average intensity between months / in a month / week / session

blocks.content: Volume and intensity change throughout the training cycle. The changes are calculated based on your profile.
Both volume and average intensity vary:

items.title: From session to session:
items.content: Each workout is different — heavy, moderate, and light weights with varying volumes.

items.title: From week to week:
items.content: Volume shifts can be dramatic. Week 1: high, week 2: low, week 3: high, week 4: moderate.

items.title: From month to month:
items.content: Long-term progression is designed to peak when it matters — competition or a PR attempt.

blocks.content: The variation follows rules. It's not random.

sections.title: WHY AND HOW DO WE WAVE LOAD?

blocks.content: Because ladders are great to manage high volumes and at the same time keep neural and metabolic fatigue low.

vision.title: VISION
vision.description: Programs are calculated first, then reviewed and adjusted by a coach. Numbers plus experience.
